species: Pacific Storm Full Spectrum -- all frequencies simultaneous, outer coast, winter
scientific_name: Meteorological event: North Pacific extratropical cyclone, outer coast landfall. No single taxonomic designation. Multiple acoustic domains: infrasound (ocean swell), low-frequency wind, mid-frequency surf, rain impact, high-frequency spray, atmospheric pressure pulse.
sound_type: All frequencies simultaneous. Six acoustic domains: (1) infrasound 0.1-1 Hz -- ocean swell compression against cliffs/sea caves; (2) low-frequency wind 20-100 Hz -- storm roar 50-80 mph sustained, 90-110 dB; (3) mid-frequency surf 100-500 Hz -- wave break impact 95-115 dB, 20-40 ft waves 12-18 sec periods; (4) rain impact 1000-5000 Hz -- heavy precipitation 1-3+ in/hr; (5) high-frequency spray/surge 5000-15000+ Hz -- wave impact on cliffs, air-water boundary; (6) very low frequency atmospheric pulse 0.01-0.1 Hz -- storm pressure system signature. E=8 through simultaneous activation of all domains, not maximum of any single domain.
ecosystem: Outer coast, Washington State Pacific shore. Rocky headlands and exposed sea stacks: Cape Flattery (48.4°N 124.7°W), Cape Alava, Point Grenville, Destruction Island. Olympic National Park coastal unit (most remote pristine coast in contiguous US). Sandy beach coast (Kalaloch south) absorbs wave energy; rocky headlands maximize acoustic output. North Pacific fetch 3000-5000 miles.
season: Winter, November-March peak storm season. North Pacific extratropical cyclone development maximum: polar-subtropical temperature gradient, polar vortex displacement, storm track aimed directly at Washington coast. Multiple events per season.
family: Meteorology. North Pacific extratropical cyclone. Bomb cyclone (explosive cyclogenesis) defined as deepening 24+ mb in 24 hours. Central pressure 950-970 mb for major events. Sustained winds 50-80 mph at coast.
milestone: SPS METAL SUMMIT OPENS. Full-spectrum acoustic event -- all frequency domains simultaneously engaged. E=8 through breadth maximum rather than amplitude maximum. Opens the SPS arc of 333-336 that matches the S36 Metal Summit energy-for-energy. Paired with These Arms Are Snakes (S36-333, E=8): both are full-system simultaneous-engagement events, not single-mechanism maxima.

coordinates.description: Cape Flattery and Olympic Peninsula outer coast: 48.4°N 124.7°W (Cape Flattery) to 47.3°N 124.4°W (Point Grenville). Olympic National Park coastal unit. Fully exposed to North Pacific. Rocky headlands for maximum acoustic output.

acoustic_data.domain_1_infrasound: Ocean swell, 0.1-1 Hz. Long-period waves 12-25 second periods. Sea cave and cliff compression. Felt in chest/abdomen below hearing threshold. Storm and swell infrasound can travel thousands of miles ahead of the storm system.
acoustic_data.domain_2_wind: Storm wind roar, 20-100 Hz. Sustained 50-80 mph. Amplitude 90-110 dB at exposed positions during peak gusts. Physically oppressive low-frequency broadband.
acoustic_data.domain_3_surf: Wave break and surge, 100-500 Hz. 95-115 dB. 20-40 ft waves at 12-18 sec periods during major storms. Continuous during storm -- overlapping surge and retreat prevent silence between individual wave events.
acoustic_data.domain_4_rain: Heavy precipitation impact, 1000-5000 Hz. 1-3+ inches per hour during intense outer coast storm bands. Individual drop impact and cumulative dense hiss.
acoustic_data.domain_5_spray: Wave impact spray and air-water boundary, 5000-15000+ Hz. Sharp high-amplitude transients during wave impact on rocky headlands. Millisecond events distinctly audible above the continuous lower-frequency sound bed.
acoustic_data.domain_6_atmospheric: Storm pressure pulse, 0.01-0.1 Hz. Very low frequency. Atmospheric pressure variation on periods of 30 sec to several minutes. Felt as pressure change. The storm system's atmospheric signature.
acoustic_data.full_spectrum_synthesis: E=8 through simultaneous activation of all six domains. Total frequency range: 0.01 Hz to 15000+ Hz. Each domain at high amplitude. Different time scales (atmospheric minutes, swell 12-25 sec, surf 12-18 sec, rain continuous, spray millisecond) create temporal complexity layered with frequency complexity. Not the maximum of any single domain -- the maximum of simultaneous breadth.

nodes.title: Full-Spectrum Storm -- Six Simultaneous Acoustic Domains
nodes.description: Outer coast Pacific winter storm activates all frequency domains simultaneously: infrasound through high-frequency spray. E=8 through breadth maximum, not amplitude maximum of any single domain.

nodes.title: Rocky Headland as Maximum Acoustic Amplifier
nodes.description: Rocky headlands (Cape Flattery, Cape Alava, Point Grenville) maximize all storm acoustic domains: wave energy reflects and concentrates rather than absorbing as on sandy beaches. Olympic National Park coastal protection maintains pristine acoustic environment.

nodes.title: 3000-5000 Mile Fetch -- Maximum Wave Energy
nodes.description: Washington outer coast receives waves from the longest fetch available on the Pacific Ocean. Swell generated by storms thousands of miles away arrives fully developed. The physics of wave growth ensures maximum swell height and period at the Washington coast.

nodes.title: Explosive Cyclogenesis -- Bomb Cyclone
nodes.description: North Pacific storms that deepen 24+ mb in 24 hours. Multiple events per winter season on the Pacific coast. These produce the most complete full-spectrum acoustic activation: all domains simultaneously at high amplitude.

cross_references.relationship: E=8 + E=8 = E=16. Post-metal's full-spectrum architectural engagement (all instruments across full dynamic range) vs. Pacific storm's full-spectrum acoustic engagement (all frequency domains simultaneously). Both operate by activating the complete range rather than maximizing a single channel.

E=9 follows E=8. Single-mechanism infrasonic event (E=9 whale+volcano) follows the full-spectrum storm event (E=8). The infrasonic deep Pacific pairing escalates from E=8 full-spectrum to E=9 single-mechanism maximum.

Both winter storm events on the outer Olympic coast. Hoh (324) is rain forest interior winter storm. Pacific Storm Full Spectrum (333) is exposed outer coast simultaneous-all-domain event. E=7 rain forest vs. E=8 exposed headland.
